subject: English
title: A Homage to our Brave Soldiers

questions.text: Where is the National War Memorial located?
questions.explanation: The National War Memorial was inaugurated in New Delhi near India Gate.

questions.text: What is the main format used in this chapter to tell the story?
questions.explanation: The chapter consists of letters written between two friends, Soumya and Ananda.

questions.text: Who wrote the first letter in the chapter?
questions.explanation: Soumya writes to Ananda after visiting the National War Memorial.

questions.text: What is the name of the 'eternal flame' at the National War Memorial?
questions.explanation: The Amar Jawan Jyoti (Flame of the Immortal Soldier) represents the eternal spirit of the soldiers.

questions.text: Which of these is the highest gallantry award in India during wartime?
questions.explanation: The Param Vir Chakra is the highest military decoration for wartime bravery.

questions.text: Which gallantry award is given for bravery during peacetime?
questions.explanation: The Ashoka Chakra is India's highest peacetime military decoration.

questions.text: The National War Memorial consists of four concentric circles. What are they called?
questions.explanation: They are known as Amar Chakra, Veerta Chakra, Tyag Chakra, and Rakshak Chakra.

questions.text: Which chakra signifies the 'Circle of Sacrifice'?
questions.explanation: Tyag Chakra represents the sacrifice of soldiers, with their names inscribed on granite tablets.

questions.text: What is inscribed on the tablets in the Tyag Chakra?
questions.explanation: Each granite brick in the Tyag Chakra bears the name of a soldier who died for the nation.

questions.text: Who was Major Somnath Sharma?
options: The first recipient of the Param Vir Chakra
questions.explanation: Major Somnath Sharma was posthumously awarded India's first Param Vir Chakra.

questions.text: Why did Soumya feel 'inspired' after visiting the memorial?
options: She learned about the selfless bravery of soldiers
questions.explanation: Learning about the stories of courage made her feel proud and motivated to serve her country.

questions.text: The 'Amar Chakra' represents:
questions.explanation: Amar means immortal; this circle contains the eternal flame.

questions.text: What was the occupation of Ananda's aunt mentioned in the letter?
questions.explanation: Ananda mentions her aunt who serves in the military and provides more information.

questions.text: Which chakra depicts the bronze murals of different battles?
questions.explanation: Veerta Chakra (Circle of Bravery) showcases major battles through murals.

questions.text: What does the 'Rakshak Chakra' represent?
questions.explanation: The outermost circle, Rakshak (Protector), symbolizes the protection of the nation.

questions.text: How many Param Vir Chakra recipients are honored at the memorial with bronze busts?
questions.explanation: The Param Yoddha Sthal has bronze busts of all 21 Param Vir Chakra recipients.

questions.text: What was Captain Vikram Batra's famous saying mentioned in the context of bravery?
questions.explanation: Captain Batra used this phrase during the Kargil War to signal success.

questions.text: The letters in the chapter highlight the values of:
questions.explanation: The entire chapter is a tribute (homage) to the spirit of the armed forces.

questions.text: Which day is celebrated as 'National Army Day' in India?
questions.explanation: January 15 is observed as Army Day annually.

questions.text: The term 'Posthumous' often used with these awards means:
questions.explanation: Many gallantry awards are given to soldiers who lost their lives in action.

questions.text: What was Soumya's reaction to reading the names on the wall?
options: She felt a deep sense of respect and sadness
questions.explanation: Seeing the thousands of names makes the human cost of freedom real for her.

questions.text: The National War Memorial is built in an area known as:
questions.explanation: It is located in the Central Vista area of New Delhi.

questions.text: Which force is NOT part of the Indian Armed Forces?
questions.explanation: The Armed Forces specifically refer to the Army, Navy, and Air Force.

questions.text: What is the Obelisk in the center of the memorial?
options: A tall stone pillar with the eternal flame
questions.explanation: The central obelisk is a 15-meter tall stone pillar representing the immortal soldier.

questions.text: Ananda's letter shows that she is ________ about her friend's visit.
questions.explanation: Ananda thanks Soumya for sharing the experience and asks more questions.

questions.text: The design of the memorial is based on a ________.
questions.explanation: It uses the concept of 'Chakras' or concentric circles.

questions.text: Which of these is a peacetime gallantry award?
questions.explanation: The Ashoka Chakra, Kirti Chakra, and Shaurya Chakra are peacetime awards.

questions.text: Who is the Supreme Commander of the Indian Armed Forces?
questions.explanation: The President of India holds this constitutional position.

questions.text: The memorial serves as a place for:
questions.explanation: It is a sacred space to honor those who died for the country.

questions.text: Soumya mentions 'India Gate'. Who built India Gate?
questions.explanation: India Gate was built by the British to honor soldiers of World War I.

questions.text: How many soldiers' names are approximately inscribed at the memorial?
questions.explanation: More than 25,000 names of martyrs since independence are inscribed there.

questions.text: The National War Memorial was dedicated to the nation in:
questions.explanation: It was inaugurated in February 2019.

questions.text: What was the name of Ananda's school friend who moved away?
questions.explanation: Soumya is the friend who moved and is writing the letter.

questions.text: The murals in Veerta Chakra are made of:
questions.explanation: Bronze is used for its durability and artistic quality in memorials.

questions.text: What feeling does the Amar Jawan Jyoti evoke?
questions.explanation: The flame symbolizes the undying spirit of the soldier.

questions.text: Why is the memorial important for children?
options: To learn about history and citizenship
questions.explanation: It teaches children about the values that protect the nation.

questions.text: Which chakra is at the very center?
questions.explanation: The Amar Chakra is the innermost circle.

questions.text: Ananda says 'Freedom is not free'. This means:
options: You have to pay money for it
Soldiers pay for it with their lives and service
questions.explanation: This profound statement acknowledges the sacrifice required to maintain a free country.

questions.text: In a letter, where do you usually write the date?
questions.explanation: Standard letter writing format puts the date at the top.

questions.text: What was the purpose of the letters between Soumya and Ananda?
options: To share a meaningful experience and learn together
questions.explanation: The exchange of letters allows them to reflect on patriotism and share knowledge.
